title: Pediatric Developmental Assessment - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Pediatric Developmental Assessment 22 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Autism Spectrum Disorder 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT TILLAMOOK SERVICE CENTER EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities. Support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, social, emotional and/or adaptive development. 2515 3rd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation, children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for the Early Intervention (EI) or Early Childhood Special Education program (ECSE) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 842-8243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Department of Education contracts with local agencies to provide a statewide system of free services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities and their families, including: -Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children birth to three and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional, and/or adaptive development; and -Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children ages 3 to the age of public school eligibility in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive, and others. 13455 SE 97th Avenue Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI/ECSE services. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 675-4041 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DAVID DOUGLAS SCHOOL DISTRICT 40 EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional, and/or adaptive development. 11300 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Ages 0-5 with a suspected developmental delay or disability Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 386-4919 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional and/or adaptive development. Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children. Support offered in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive and others. 1409 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Suite 110 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI and ECSE services Hours: Monday-Friday 8am to 4pm Email (541) 440-4794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT JACKSON COUNTY EI/ECSE PROGRAM EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional and/or adaptive development. These services support districts as they work to build the capacity of their special education teams. Examples of this work include developing staff knowledge of the characteristics of ASD, classroom-based strategies, evidence-based practices (EBPs), and assessment requirements and procedures. 5825 NE Ray Circle Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Ages 5-21, medical or health assessment is required as part of the educational eligibility process Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 614-1310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CLATSOP SERVICE CENTER EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities. Support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, social, emotional and/or adaptive development. 800 Port Avenue Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation, children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for the Early Intervention (EI) or Early Childhood Special Education program (ECSE) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 366-1251 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON PROVIDENCE CHILD CENTER PROVIDENCE CHILDREN'S DEVELOPMENT INSTITUTE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides physical, occupational, and speech therapies, autism therapy, feeding and nutrition services, audiology, and neurodevelopmental assessments. 830 NE 47th Avenue Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-5:30pm (503) 215-2240 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNIVERSITY OF OREGON UNIVERSITY OF OREGON HEDCO CLINIC COMPREHENSIVE DIAGNOSTIC ASSESSMENT CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers strength-based, comprehensive evaluations for Attention-Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) and learning disabilities (including dyslexia, dyscalculia, and dysgraphia).
